title: Execution Context
summary: JavaScript execution context represents the setup phase for code execution, consisting of Global Execution Context (GEC) for global scope and Functional Execution Context (FEC) for individual functions. JavaScript processes code in two phases: Creation phase (memory allocation and hoisting) and Execution phase (actual code execution via the call stack). Understanding these contexts is fundamental to comprehending how JavaScript manages variable scope and function execution order.
content:
Bayangkan Execution Context sebuah dapur. 
sebelum kamu memulai memasak(mengeksekusi code)
kamu perlu ruang kerja, peralatan(variable)
resep(functions)
jadi disini Execution context adalah tahap persiapan atau "set up" tersebut.
perlu kamu ketahui JavaScript tidak menjalankan kode dalam satu tumpukan raksasa yang berantakan. Ia membuat konteks yang spesifik:
Global Execution Context (GEC):Ini adalah kotak default. Jika kode Kamu tidak berada di dalam fungsi, maka ia ada di sini. GEC membuat dua hal secara otomatis: objek window (di browser) dan kata kunci spesial this.


Functional Execution Context (FEC): Setiap kali Kamu memanggil fungsi, JavaScript membuat kotak mini baru khusus untuk fungsi tersebut. Di sinilah variabel dan logika spesifik tugas tersebut disimpan.




  
  
  Second, The Two Phases (The "Magic" Trick)


JavaScript tidak sekadar membaca kode baris-demi-baris dari atas ke bawah dan langsung mengeksekusinya. Ia melakukan dua kali lintasan di dalam "kotak" Kamu.secara instan. 
Fase A: Fase Penciptaan (Alokasi Memori)
Membuat Objek Global (window).
Menyiapkan Ruang Memori untuk variabel dan fungsi.
Hoisting: Ia mengatur deklarasi variabel menjadi undefined dan menyimpan seluruh deklarasi fungsi ke dalam memori.
Fase B: Fase Eksekusi
Karena JavaScript bersifat single-threaded (hanya bisa melakukan satu hal dalam satu waktu), ia menggunakan Call Stack untuk melacak "kotak" mana yang sedang dikerjakan.
Bayangkan setumpuk keripik Pringles:
Global Context adalah keripik paling bawah.
Saat Anda memanggil fungsi A(), keripik baru (Context A) diletakkan di atasnya.
Jika fungsi A memanggil fungsi B, Context B diletakkan di atas A.
Setelah fungsi B selesai, ia "dibuang" (popped) dari tumpukan. Kita kembali ke A.

zh.title: 执行上下文
zh.summary: JavaScript执行上下文代表代码执行的设置阶段，包括全局执行上下文(GEC)用于全局作用域和函数执行上下文(FEC)用于单个函数。JavaScript分两个阶段处理代码：创建阶段(内存分配和提升)和执行阶段(通过调用栈实际执行代码)。理解这些上下文对于理解JavaScript如何管理变量作用域和函数执行顺序至关重要。

fr.title: Contexte d'exécution
fr.summary: Le contexte d'exécution JavaScript représente la phase de configuration de l'exécution du code, composée du contexte d'exécution global (GEC) pour la portée globale et du contexte d'exécution fonctionnel (FEC) pour les fonctions individuelles. JavaScript traite le code en deux phases : la phase de création (allocation de mémoire et hoisting) et la phase d'exécution (exécution réelle du code via la pile d'appels). Comprendre ces contextes est fondamental pour comprendre comment JavaScript gère la portée des variables et l'ordre d'exécution des fonctions.

de.title: Ausführungskontext
de.summary: Der JavaScript-Ausführungskontext stellt die Einrichtungsphase für die Code-Ausführung dar und besteht aus dem globalen Ausführungskontext (GEC) für den globalen Gültigkeitsbereich und dem funktionalen Ausführungskontext (FEC) für einzelne Funktionen. JavaScript verarbeitet Code in zwei Phasen: Erstellungsphase (Speicherallokation und Hoisting) und Ausführungsphase (tatsächliche Code-Ausführung über den Call Stack). Das Verständnis dieser Kontexte ist grundlegend für das Verständnis, wie JavaScript den Variablenumfang und die Funktionsausführungsreihenfolge verwaltet.

es.title: Contexto de ejecución
es.summary: El contexto de ejecución de JavaScript representa la fase de configuración para la ejecución del código, consistiendo en el Contexto de Ejecución Global (GEC) para el ámbito global y el Contexto de Ejecución Funcional (FEC) para funciones individuales. JavaScript procesa el código en dos fases: fase de creación (asignación de memoria y hoisting) y fase de ejecución (ejecución real del código a través de la pila de llamadas). Entender estos contextos es fundamental para comprender cómo JavaScript gestiona el alcance de las variables y el orden de ejecución de las funciones.

title: Privacy First: Running Llama-3 Locally in Your Browser for Medical Report Analysis via WebGPU
summary: This article demonstrates building a privacy-focused, browser-based medical document analyzer using Llama-3 and WebGPU, eliminating the need to send sensitive health data to cloud services. The solution leverages WebGPU acceleration for local GPU inference of a quantized 8B parameter LLM, enabling real-time medical report analysis entirely within the user's browser. This approach combines edge computing with React and TypeScript to provide AI-powered insights while maintaining complete data privacy.
content:
We’ve all been there—staring at a complex medical report filled with cryptic numbers and Latin terminology. Our first instinct is to paste it into ChatGPT. But wait... do you really want your sensitive health data sitting on a corporate server forever? 
In the era of WebGPU acceleration and local LLM inference, you no longer have to choose between AI power and data privacy. Today, we are building a browser-based AI medical analyzer. By leveraging Llama-3 WebLLM and edge computing, we will transform a quantized 8B parameter model into a local powerhouse that processes medical documents with zero data leakage.
If you are looking for more production-ready patterns on data privacy and AI, be sure to check out the advanced guides over at WellAlly Tech Blog, which served as a major inspiration for this local-first architecture. 
The magic happens through the WebGPU API, which allows the browser to tap directly into your device's GPU. Unlike traditional WebGL, WebGPU is designed for modern compute workloads, making it perfect for running 4-bit quantized LLMs.
graph TD
    A[User Uploads Health Report] --> B[React Frontend]
    B --> C{WebGPU Support?}
    C -- Yes --> D[WebLLM Engine Initialization]
    C -- No --> E[Fallback: CPU/WASM or Error]
    D --> F[Load Llama-3-8B-q4f16_1]
    F --> G[Local Inference]
    G --> H[Structured JSON Output]
    H --> I[UI Display & Visualization]
    subgraph Browser_Sandbox
    D
    F
    G
    end

To follow this "Learning in Public" journey, you’ll need:
Tech Stack: React (Vite), TypeScript, WebLLM.
Hardware: A device with a modern GPU (M1/M2/M3 Mac, or RTX-series Windows).
Browser: Chrome/Edge (v113+) with WebGPU enabled.
First, we need to set up the engine that manages the model weights and the WebGPU pipeline. We'll use Llama-3-8B-Instruct-v0.1-q4f16_1-MLC, a version optimized for the web.
// engine.ts
import { CreateMLCEngine, MLCEngine } from "@mlc-ai/web-llm";

export async function initEngine(onProgress: (p: number) => void

zh.title: 隐私优先：在您的浏览器中本地运行Llama-3进行医疗报告分析via WebGPU
zh.summary: 本文演示了使用Llama-3和WebGPU构建一个注重隐私的、基于浏览器的医疗文件分析器，消除了将敏感健康数据发送到云服务的需要。该解决方案利用WebGPU加速进行量化的8B参数LLM的本地GPU推理，能够在用户浏览器内完全进行实时医疗报告分析。这种方法将边缘计算与React和TypeScript相结合，以提供AI驱动的见解，同时保持完整的数据隐私。

fr.title: Confidentialité d'abord : exécuter Llama-3 localement dans votre navigateur pour l'analyse de rapports médicaux via WebGPU
fr.summary: Cet article démontre la construction d'un analyseur de documents médicaux basé sur le navigateur et axé sur la confidentialité en utilisant Llama-3 et WebGPU, éliminant le besoin d'envoyer des données sanitaires sensibles à des services cloud. La solution exploite l'accélération WebGPU pour l'inférence GPU locale d'un LLM quantifié de 8B paramètres, permettant l'analyse de rapports médicaux en temps réel entièrement dans le navigateur de l'utilisateur. Cette approche combine l'informatique en périphérie avec React et TypeScript pour fournir des insights alimentés par l'IA tout en maintenant une confidentialité complète des données.

de.title: Datenschutz an erster Stelle: Llama-3 lokal in Ihrem Browser ausführen für medizinische Berichtanalyse via WebGPU
de.summary: Dieser Artikel demonstriert die Erstellung eines datenschutzorientierten, browserbasierten Analysators für medizinische Dokumente unter Verwendung von Llama-3 und WebGPU, wodurch die Notwendigkeit entfällt, sensible Gesundheitsdaten an Cloud-Services zu senden. Die Lösung nutzt die WebGPU-Beschleunigung für lokale GPU-Inferenz eines quantisierten 8B-Parameter-LLM und ermöglicht die Echtzeit-Analyse von medizinischen Berichten vollständig im Browser des Benutzers. Dieser Ansatz kombiniert Edge-Computing mit React und TypeScript, um KI-gestützte Erkenntnisse zu liefern und gleichzeitig vollständigen Datenschutz zu wahren.

es.title: Privacidad primero: ejecutar Llama-3 localmente en su navegador para análisis de informes médicos a través de WebGPU
es.summary: Este artículo demuestra la construcción de un analizador de documentos médicos basado en navegador y enfocado en privacidad utilizando Llama-3 y WebGPU, eliminando la necesidad de enviar datos de salud sensibles a servicios en la nube. La solución aprovecha la aceleración de WebGPU para la inferencia de GPU local de un LLM cuantificado de 8B parámetros, permitiendo análisis de informes médicos en tiempo real completamente dentro del navegador del usuario. Este enfoque combina computación perimetral con React y TypeScript para proporcionar información impulsada por IA mientras mantiene la privacidad completa de los datos.

title: I stopped copy-pasting my lint rules into 12 different config files
summary: The author developed crag, a tool that consolidates lint, test, and build rules from a single governance.md file into 12 downstream configuration files automatically, eliminating manual synchronization across CI workflows and editor configurations. This deterministic, zero-dependency tool generates consistent configurations across multiple platforms and prevents configuration drift. The tool has been stress-tested across 101 open-source repositories with 4,400 invocations and zero failures.
content:
Every project I work on has the same problem: my lint/test/build rules exist in my CI workflow, my pre-commit hook, my .cursorrules, my AGENTS.md, my GEMINI.md, and 7 more files. When I change a rule in one place, the others drift.
I built crag to fix this.
You write one ~20-line governance.md. crag compiles it to 12 downstream files atomically:
.github/workflows/gates.yml (GitHub Actions)
.husky/pre-commit
.pre-commit-config.yaml
AGENTS.md (Codex, Aider, Factory)
.cursor/rules/governance.mdc
GEMINI.md
.github/copilot-instructions.md
.clinerules, .continuerules, .windsurfrules

.zed/rules.md
.sourcegraph/cody-instructions.md
Change one line, regenerate everything.
npx @whitehatd/crag demo

No install. No config. No network. No LLM. ~500ms to a verified

512 tests passing across Ubuntu + macOS + Windows × Node 18/20/22
Stress-tested on 101 OSS repos (4,400 invocations, 0 crashes)
Reference benchmark: 40/40 Grade A on ship-ready governance
Deterministic: same input → byte-identical output, SHA-verified on every CI push across 9 runners
Zero runtime dependencies
GitHub: https://github.com/WhitehatD/crag
https://www.npmjs.com/package/@whitehatd/crag
MIT licensed. If you use more than one AI coding tool alongside CI,


Tags: devtools, opensource, javascript, ai

La herramienta ha sido probada bajo estrés en 101 repositorios de código abierto con 4.400 invocaciones y cero fallos.

title: Harness Engineering: 5 Companies, 5 Definitions -- Why Everyone Disagrees on What It Means
summary: After OpenAI's February 2026 publication on harness engineering, the term exploded but definitions diverge significantly across organizations. OpenAI emphasizes declarative constraint systems for agent-driven automation at scale, while Anthropic focuses on managing context anxiety and maintaining agent stability through periodic resets. All definitions share a common nesting structure: Harness ⊃ Context ⊃ Prompt, but companies diverge substantially on implementation priorities.
content:
"Harness Engineering" Has a Definition Problem


In February 2026, OpenAI published "Harness engineering: leveraging Codex in an agent-first world," and the term exploded overnight.
Within weeks, Anthropic released two guides. LangChain defined it on their official blog. Birgitta Böckeler wrote a deep analysis on martinfowler.com. An arXiv paper formalized the concept.
But here's the thing: they're all saying slightly different things.
Same word. Different metaphors. Different starting points. Different conclusions.
I read all five. Here's what I found.

There's a nesting structure no one disputes:
Harness ⊃ Context ⊃ Prompt
SmartScope's article captures it best:
Writing "run the linter" in CLAUDE.md versus enforcing linter execution via hooks is the difference between "almost every time" and "every time, no exceptions."
Beyond this? It gets messy.
OpenAI's article dropped a bombshell.
For 5 months, their engineers wrote zero lines of code. Over 1 million lines of production application code, all built by Codex agents. Build time: 1/10th of handwritten code.
"Humans steer. Agents execute."
For OpenAI, a harness is a declarative constraint system. You describe "what should be." The agent figures out "how."
Focus areas:
Scaling massive projects
Parallel agent execution
Sandboxed safety guarantees
Anthropic took a completely different starting point.
Where OpenAI started with "let's automate an entire large-scale project," Anthropic started with "how do we keep a long-running agent stable?"
Their unique concept: context anxiety. When the context window fills up with information, model output quality degrades. Like a human in a 3-hour meeting with no agenda -- the AI starts making worse decisions.
With Claude Sonnet 4.5, context anxiety was strong enough that compaction (summarization) alone could not maintain performance on long tasks. Context resets became essential.
Their solution: periodic resets, with claude-progress.txt and git history carrying state to the next s

zh.title: 束缚工程：5家公司，5种定义——为什么大家对其含义存在分歧
zh.summary: OpenAI在2026年2月发布的束缚工程论文发表后，这个术语快速传播，但不同组织的定义差异显著。OpenAI强调用于代理驱动的大规模自动化的声明式约束系统，而Anthropic则专注于管理上下文焦虑并通过定期重置保持代理稳定性。所有定义共享一个通用嵌套结构：束缚⊃上下文⊃提示，但公司在实现优先级上差异很大。

fr.title: Ingénierie de Harnais : 5 Entreprises, 5 Définitions — Pourquoi Tout le Monde Est en Désaccord sur Leur Signification
fr.summary: Après la publication d'OpenAI en février 2026 sur l'ingénierie de harnais, le terme a explosé mais les définitions divergent considérablement selon les organisations. OpenAI met l'accent sur les systèmes de contraintes déclaratifs pour l'automatisation d'agents à grande échelle, tandis qu'Anthropic se concentre sur la gestion de l'anxiété contextuelle et le maintien de la stabilité des agents par des réinitialisations périodiques. Toutes les définitions partagent une structure imbriquée commune : Harnais ⊃ Contexte ⊃ Invite, mais les entreprises divergent considérablement sur les priorités de mise en œuvre.

de.title: Harness-Engineering: 5 Unternehmen, 5 Definitionen — Warum sich alle über die Bedeutung uneinig sind
de.summary: Nach OpenAIs Veröffentlichung im Februar 2026 zur Harness-Engineering ist der Begriff explodiert, aber die Definitionen divergieren erheblich zwischen Organisationen. OpenAI betont deklarative Constraint-Systeme für agentengesteuerte Automatisierung in großem Maßstab, während Anthropic sich auf die Verwaltung von Kontextangst und die Aufrechterhaltung der Agentstabilität durch regelmäßige Zurückstellungen konzentriert. Alle Definitionen teilen eine gemeinsame verschachtelte Struktur: Harness ⊃ Kontext ⊃ Eingabeaufforderung, aber Unternehmen unterscheiden sich erheblich in den Implementierungsprioritäten.

es.title: Ingeniería de Arnés: 5 Empresas, 5 Definiciones — Por Qué Todos Discrepan Sobre Su Significado
es.summary: Después de la publicación de OpenAI en febrero de 2026 sobre ingeniería de arnés, el término explotó pero las definiciones divergen significativamente entre organizaciones. OpenAI enfatiza sistemas de restricciones declarativos para automatización impulsada por agentes a escala, mientras que Anthropic se enfoca en gestionar la ansiedad contextual y mantener la estabilidad del agente mediante reinicializaciones periódicas. Todas las definiciones comparten una estructura de anidamiento común: Arnés ⊃ Contexto ⊃ Indicación, pero las empresas divergen sustancialmente en las prioridades de implementación.

title: Cx Dev Log — 2026-04-05
summary: The Cx development log documents branch housekeeping with PRs #28 and #30 merging daily logs and roadmap updates to main, maintaining the compiler/runtime test score at 78/78. The submain branch holds five high-value commits passing 82/82 tests but remains unmerged despite 10 consecutive days of readiness, indicating a non-technical holdup. Multiple daily-log branches await merging once submain integration completes.
content:
Merging branches isn't usually the most thrilling part of a project, but sometimes it's crucial to understand the bigger picture. Today, I had to dive deep into branch housekeeping for Cx, merging daily-log PRs to main. Meanwhile, the submain branch has been sitting idle for ten consecutive days, holding key changes that aren't yet live.
Two important PRs made their way to main today, pushing forward our progress on daily logs and the roadmap. PR #28 brought the 2026-03-28 daily log and a v4.8 roadmap bump into main. Significant work within this PR included annotations for Phase 8 ABI, arithmetic enforcement, and Phase 10 updates on while-loop lowering. PR #30 followed close behind, merging the 2026-03-30 daily log into main.
Over on the site branch, we committed a 2026-04-04 blog post, focusing on changes to the test runner and the Result design. The 2026-03-29 branch has also been synced with main. There's a good chance it will be the next candidate for merging. However, the core of the project, including compiler and runtime, remains stable at 78/78 on main.
Now, here's where things get interesting. The submain branch holds five commits that stretch over eight days’ worth of work but are yet to land on main. These include:
d93582b - wrapping arithmetic enforcement
0fa06a6 - Phase 10 while loop lowering
09ef2a3 - UTF-8 decision locked
e214d15 - semicolon rule refinement
762b188 - test runner
Collectively, these commits resolve four significant blockers: test runner, UTF-8, integer overflow, and semicolons, all passing tests on submain with a score of 82/82. Meanwhile, main sits steady at 78/78. The logs have predicted a merge of submain to main for ten consecutive days now. Yet, it hasn't happened. All tests pass, the work is verified; the holdup isn't technical.
Even with today's merges, we still have five daily-log branches waiting their turn: 2026-03-29, and daily logs from 2026-04-01 through 2026-04-04. With 2026-03-29 now synced, it's poised for the next merg

zh.title: Cx开发日志 — 2026-04-05
zh.summary: Cx开发日志记录了分支管理工作，PR #28和#30将每日日志和路线图更新合并到主分支，保持编译器/运行时测试得分为78/78。子主分支保持五个高价值提交，通过82/82测试，但尽管连续10天已准备好，仍未合并，表明存在非技术性障碍。一旦子主分支集成完成，多个每日日志分支等待合并。

La branche submain contient cinq commits de grande valeur passant 82/82 tests mais reste non fusionnée malgré 10 jours consécutifs de préparation, indiquant un problème non technique. Plusieurs branches de journaux quotidiens attendent la fusion une fois l'intégration submain terminée.

de.title: Cx-Entwicklungsprotokoll — 2026-04-05
de.summary: Das Cx-Entwicklungsprotokoll dokumentiert die Branch-Verwaltung mit PRs #28 und #30, die tägliche Protokolle und Roadmap-Updates in den Hauptbranch zusammenführen, wobei der Compiler-/Runtime-Testwert bei 78/78 bleibt. Der Submain-Branch enthält fünf hochwertige Commits, die 82/82 Tests bestehen, bleibt aber trotz 10 aufeinanderfolgender Tage Bereitschaft nicht zusammengeführt, was auf ein nicht-technisches Problem hindeutet. Mehrere tägliche Log-Branches warten auf die Zusammenführung, sobald die Submain-Integration abgeschlossen ist.

title: BiRefNet vs rembg vs U2Net: Which Background Removal Model Actually Works in Production?
summary: A production comparison of three background removal models across 500 real-world images found BiRefNet significantly outperforms rembg and U2Net, achieving 94% hair accuracy versus rembg's 81% and U2Net's 71%. BiRefNet's superior handling of transparent objects and complex fabrics results in far fewer manual touch-ups at scale, justifying the slower inference time (1.4s vs 1.1s). This quality gap directly impacts production economics for large-volume image processing.
content:
BiRefNet vs rembg vs U2Net: Which Background Removal Model Actually Works in Production?


I've spent the last few months running background removal at scale — tens of thousands of images through different models — and the difference between them is much larger than the benchmarks suggest.
Here's the honest breakdown.
Background removal sounds like a solved problem. It isn't.
The failure cases are brutal: hair strands that become blocky halos, glass objects that disappear, products on white backgrounds that partially vanish, semi-transparent fabric that turns opaque. Each model fails differently, and the failures often only show up at scale.
rembg — the classic. Wraps ISNet and U2Net under a unified API. Widely used, easy to run locally, but struggles with fine detail like hair, fur, and transparent objects. Good for simple product shots with clear subject-background contrast.
U2Net — the academic ancestor. Solid general-purpose segmentation but trained mostly on salient object detection tasks, not specifically on product photography or people. Fast, low VRAM.
BiRefNet — state of the art as of 2025. Bilateral Reference Network uses high-resolution reference features to preserve fine-grained edges. Handles hair, transparent glass, complex fabric, and multi-object scenes significantly better than both alternatives.
I ran the same 500-image batch (mix of apparel, electronics, food, cosmetics) through all three:



Model
Hair accuracy
Glass/transparent
Avg inference
Overall quality




U2Net
71%
48%
0.8s
Acceptable


rembg/ISNet
81%
59%
1.1s
Good


BiRefNet
94%
78%
1.4s
Excellent



These aren't cherry-picked. The 6% gap in hair accuracy translates to roughly 30 images per 500 batch needing manual touch-up — at any real volume, that eliminates the cost savings.
Running rembg locally:
from rembg import remove
from PIL import Image
import io

input_image = Image.open("product.jpg")
output = remove(input_image)
output.save("output.png")

Works fine locally. The catch: remb

zh.title: BiRefNet对阵rembg对阵U2Net：哪个背景去除模型在生产环境中真正有效？
zh.summary: 在500张真实图像上的生产比较发现BiRefNet的性能明显优于rembg和U2Net，头发精确度达94%，而rembg为81%，U2Net为71%。BiRefNet在处理透明物体和复杂织物方面优越，导致大规模情况下手动修饰大幅减少，这证明了推理时间的增加（1.4秒对1.1秒）是合理的。这种质量差异直接影响大容量图像处理的生产经济学。

fr.title: BiRefNet vs rembg vs U2Net : Quel Modèle de Suppression d'Arrière-plan Fonctionne Réellement en Production ?
fr.summary: Une comparaison de production de trois modèles de suppression d'arrière-plan sur 500 images réelles a révélé que BiRefNet surpasse considérablement rembg et U2Net, atteignant une précision des cheveux de 94% contre 81% pour rembg et 71% pour U2Net. La manipulation supérieure de BiRefNet des objets transparents et des tissus complexes entraîne beaucoup moins de retouches manuelles à grande échelle, justifiant le temps d'inférence plus lent (1,4 s contre 1,1 s). Cet écart de qualité a un impact direct sur l'économie de la production pour le traitement d'images en grand volume.

de.title: BiRefNet gegen rembg gegen U2Net: Welches Hintergrundentfernungsmodell funktioniert tatsächlich in der Produktion?
de.summary: Ein Produktionsvergleich von drei Hintergrundentfernungsmodellen auf 500 echten Bildern ergab, dass BiRefNet rembg und U2Net erheblich übertrifft und eine Haargenauigkeit von 94% gegenüber rembg mit 81% und U2Net mit 71% erreicht. BiRefNets überlegenes Handling von transparenten Objekten und komplexen Stoffen führt zu weitaus weniger manuellen Retuschen im großen Maßstab und rechtfertigt die langsamere Inferenzzeit (1,4 s gegenüber 1,1 s). Diese Qualitätslücke hat direkte Auswirkungen auf die Produktionswirtschaft für die Verarbeitung großer Bildmengen.

es.title: BiRefNet vs rembg vs U2Net: ¿Cuál Modelo de Eliminación de Fondo Realmente Funciona en Producción?
es.summary: Una comparación de producción de tres modelos de eliminación de fondo en 500 imágenes reales encontró que BiRefNet supera significativamente a rembg y U2Net, logrando una precisión del cabello del 94% frente al 81% de rembg y 71% de U2Net. El manejo superior de BiRefNet de objetos transparentes y tejidos complejos resulta en muchos menos retoques manuales a escala, justificando el tiempo de inferencia más lento (1,4 s frente a 1,1 s). Esta brecha de calidad impacta directamente la economía de producción para el procesamiento de imágenes en gran volumen.

title: From Inbox to Character: Building a Private, Local AI Email Agent
summary: The author built Llamail, a private local AI email agent processing 18,000+ personal emails using a Llama model on a consumer GPU without relying on cloud APIs. The ~3,700-line Python system operates entirely locally to protect sensitive email content and avoid API costs while providing semantic search, summarization, and draft generation through a Telegram bot interface. This project prioritizes privacy and independence as a practical alternative to cloud-based email AI services.
content:
There are 18k+ emails in my personal inbox, and it's only one of the accounts I have. I wanted to search through them semantically, get AI summaries, draft replies, and run email campaigns - all from my phone. I didn't want OpenAI reading my emails or Google's AI. Or anyone's. For me, local AI is the only real answer for private data processing because no one can read your data or train their models on your data while you're paying for the service.
So I built my own - a private, local email agent project called Llamail. Its default synthetic persona is Sable. It helps me search, summarize, and manage my emails, and can also chat in a casual, roleplay-like manner to make the whole thing a bit more fun. ~3700 lines of Python, a Llama model running on my average, consumer laptop GPU, and a Telegram bot as the interface - and everything is handcrafted, not vibecoded. Here's how.

Meet Sable - a private, local email agent with a configurable synthetic persona.

Two common workflows: RAG-based search and live incoming email processing.
This is the first article in a five-part series.
I could've used OpenAI or Gemini or any other cloud API and been done in a weekend. But emails are personal and they contain contracts, salary discussions, medical stuff, conversations with people who didn't consent to having their words processed by a third-party AI, and I wasn't comfortable with that.
There's also the practical side: 18,000+ emails means real API costs. With GPT-5.4 pricing as of April 5, 2026 ($2.50/1M input, $15/1M output tokens), the initial import alone would likely land somewhere around $60–120 depending on average email length and summary size - not ruinous on its own, but it's still an upfront cost that scales with the size of the mailbox. Then there are search queries, draft generation, and follow-up questions - it adds up. A local LLM costs electricity and patience, but it doesn't bill you per token. And honestly, I just prefer running things locally. The independe

zh.title: 从收件箱到角色: 构建私密本地AI电子邮件代理
zh.summary: 作者构建了Llamail，一个在消费级GPU上使用Llama模型处理18000多封个人电子邮件的私密本地AI电子邮件代理，无需依赖云API。这个约3700行的Python系统完全本地运行，保护敏感电子邮件内容并避免API成本，同时通过Telegram机器人界面提供语义搜索、摘要和草稿生成功能。该项目优先考虑隐私和独立性，是云基AI电子邮件服务的实用替代方案。

fr.title: De la boîte de réception au personnage: Construire un agent de messagerie IA local et privé
fr.summary: L'auteur a construit Llamail, un agent de messagerie IA local et privé traitant plus de 18 000 courriels personnels à l'aide d'un modèle Llama sur un GPU grand public sans dépendre des API cloud. Le système Python d'environ 3 700 lignes fonctionne entièrement en local pour protéger le contenu sensible des courriels et éviter les frais d'API tout en fournissant la recherche sémantique, la synthèse et la génération de brouillons via une interface de bot Telegram. Ce projet privilégie la confidentialité et l'indépendance comme alternative pratique aux services d'IA de messagerie basés sur le cloud.

de.title: Von der Inbox zum Charakter: Aufbau eines privaten, lokalen KI-E-Mail-Agenten
de.summary: Der Autor hat Llamail entwickelt, einen privaten lokalen KI-E-Mail-Agenten, der über 18.000 persönliche E-Mails mit einem Llama-Modell auf einer Consumer-GPU verarbeitet, ohne sich auf Cloud-APIs zu verlassen. Das System mit etwa 3.700 Zeilen Python läuft vollständig lokal, um sensible E-Mail-Inhalte zu schützen und API-Kosten zu vermeiden, während es durch eine Telegram-Bot-Schnittstelle semantische Suche, Zusammenfassung und Entwurfsgenerierung bereitstellt. Dieses Projekt priorisiert Datenschutz und Unabhängigkeit als praktische Alternative zu Cloud-basierten E-Mail-KI-Diensten.

es.title: De la bandeja de entrada al personaje: Construyendo un agente de correo electrónico IA privado y local
es.summary: El autor construyó Llamail, un agente de correo electrónico IA privado y local que procesa más de 18.000 correos electrónicos personales utilizando un modelo Llama en una GPU de consumo sin depender de las API de la nube. El sistema Python de aproximadamente 3.700 líneas funciona completamente de forma local para proteger el contenido sensible del correo electrónico y evitar costos de API, mientras proporciona búsqueda semántica, resumen y generación de borradores a través de una interfaz de bot de Telegram. Este proyecto prioriza la privacidad e independencia como una alternativa práctica a los servicios de IA de correo electrónico basados en la nube.

title: Running Gemma 4 Locally with Ollama and OpenCode
summary: This tutorial covers deploying Google's Gemma 4 locally using Ollama with emphasis on configuring the context window size to prevent token truncation in OpenCode integration. The default 4K context window is often consumed entirely by system prompts, causing models to ignore user input; the solution involves creating a custom Gemma 4 variant with larger context (e.g., 32K) via Ollama parameters. Proper context configuration is essential for stable local LLM operation in coding applications.
content:
First steps:


The usual first step with getting Gemma 4 running on Ollama is to pull the model:
ollama pull gemma4:e4b
See the available models and select the correct version for your system.
The e4b variant is a good starting point if your hardware can support it.
Use the ollama list command to ensure your version is now available to Ollama.
Now, run the model to ensure it works as expected:
ollama run gemma4:e4b
Ask a simple question or just say "Hello", then use /bye to exit.
Immediately run ollama ps.  You should see something like this:
NAME          ID              SIZE     PROCESSOR    CONTEXT    UNTIL              
gemma4:e4b    c6eb396dbd59    10 GB    100% GPU     4096       4 minutes from now  

Pay close attention to that CONTEXT value.  If you see 4096 like this, then Ollama is using the default 4K context window.  This will bite you when you try to work with the model in OpenCode. Symptoms of the small context window might be the model constantly stating "Just let me know what you want to do", or similar.  The cause is the system prompts eat up the bulk of that available context space and your prompt gets truncated, skipped.  To fix this we need to use a larger context window.
OpenCode has support for specifying a context window size in its configuration, but I have not seen that work with Ollama based models.  Instead, we need to set a different context window size within Ollama.  We create a new version of the model with the desired context size, then use that model in OpenCode.  The easiest way to do this is by using Ollama itself.
ollama run gemma4:e4b

# then within the Ollama model prompt run these commands:
/set parameter num_ctx 32768
/save gemma4:e4b-32k
/bye

# then confirm the new model is in place
ollama list

A note about the num_ctx value:  It should be divisible by 2, or more to the point it should be a power of 2.  In this case a 32K context window is '32768' bytes.  You can experiment for your use case/needs to see if a smaller 16K wi

zh.title: 使用Ollama和OpenCode在本地运行Gemma 4
zh.summary: 本教程涵盖使用Ollama本地部署Google的Gemma 4，重点是配置上下文窗口大小以防止OpenCode集成中的令牌截断。默认的4K上下文窗口经常被系统提示完全消耗，导致模型忽略用户输入；解决方案涉及通过Ollama参数创建具有更大上下文（例如32K）的自定义Gemma 4变体。正确的上下文配置对于编码应用中稳定的本地LLM操作至关重要。

fr.title: Exécution de Gemma 4 en local avec Ollama et OpenCode
fr.summary: Ce tutoriel couvre le déploiement local du Gemma 4 de Google à l'aide d'Ollama en mettant l'accent sur la configuration de la taille de la fenêtre contextuelle pour éviter la troncature des jetons dans l'intégration OpenCode. La fenêtre contextuelle par défaut de 4K est souvent entièrement consommée par les invites système, ce qui amène les modèles à ignorer l'entrée de l'utilisateur; la solution consiste à créer une variante Gemma 4 personnalisée avec un contexte plus large (par exemple 32K) via les paramètres d'Ollama. La configuration correcte du contexte est essentielle pour un fonctionnement LLM local stable dans les applications de codage.

de.title: Lokales Ausführen von Gemma 4 mit Ollama und OpenCode
de.summary: Dieses Tutorial behandelt die lokale Bereitstellung von Googles Gemma 4 mit Ollama mit Fokus auf die Konfiguration der Kontextfenstergröße, um eine Token-Kürzung in der OpenCode-Integration zu vermeiden. Das Standard-4K-Kontextfenster wird oft vollständig durch Systemaufforderungen verbraucht, was dazu führt, dass Modelle Benutzereingaben ignorieren; die Lösung besteht darin, eine benutzerdefinierte Gemma-4-Variante mit größerem Kontext (z. B. 32K) über Ollama-Parameter zu erstellen. Eine ordnungsgemäße Kontextkonfiguration ist für stabilen lokalen LLM-Betrieb in Coding-Anwendungen unerlässlich.

es.title: Ejecutar Gemma 4 localmente con Ollama y OpenCode
es.summary: Este tutorial cubre la implementación local de Gemma 4 de Google usando Ollama con énfasis en configurar el tamaño de la ventana de contexto para evitar el truncamiento de tokens en la integración de OpenCode. La ventana de contexto predeterminada de 4K a menudo se consume completamente por indicaciones del sistema, lo que hace que los modelos ignoren la entrada del usuario; la solución implica crear una variante personalizada de Gemma 4 con un contexto más grande (por ejemplo, 32K) a través de parámetros de Ollama. La configuración correcta del contexto es esencial para un funcionamiento LLM local estable en aplicaciones de codificación.

title: Budget Breakdown: $100K Salary After Taxes — You're Not Rich
summary: A $100K gross salary yields only $78,736 annual take-home in zero-tax states (or $73,000-$74,000 in high-tax states) after federal income tax ($13,614), FICA taxes ($7,650), and state income taxes. This breaks down to approximately $6,561/month pre-tax in favorable states or roughly $6,083-$6,167/month in high-tax areas, before rent and living expenses. The analysis demonstrates that six-figure salaries provide comfortable middle-class living rather than wealth.
content:
You finally hit six figures. You updated your LinkedIn, told your mom, maybe even test-drove a BMW. Then your first $100K paycheck landed: $6,250. Before rent. Before your student loans. Before the $19 oat milk latte habit you swore you'd quit.
Here's the truth nobody celebrating "100K club" on Reddit wants to hear — you're not rich. You're barely comfortable. And this budget breakdown of a 100K salary after taxes is going to prove it with real numbers you can't argue with.
Let's rip the band-aid off.
You earn $100,000 gross. Uncle Sam doesn't care about your BMW fantasy. Here's what actually happens to your money, using the 2025 federal tax brackets for a single filer taking the standard deduction.
Step 1: Standard Deduction
The 2025 standard deduction for single filers is $15,000. That drops your taxable income to $85,000.
Step 2: Federal Income Tax (Progressive Brackets)
Your $85,000 in taxable income gets sliced up like this:



Bracket
Amount
Tax




10% on the first $11,925
$11,925
$1,192.50


12% on $11,925 – $48,475
$36,550
$4,386.00


22% on $48,475 – $85,000
$36,525
$8,035.50



Total federal income tax: $13,614
Your effective federal tax rate? Just 13.6%. Not the 22% marginal rate people panic about. But $13,614 is still gone before you see a dime.
Step 3: FICA Taxes (The One Nobody Talks About)
Social Security (6.2%) and Medicare (1.45%) hit your entire $100,000 gross — no deduction, no shelter, no escape.
FICA: $100,000 × 7.65% = $7,650



Step 4: State Income Tax
This is where it gets ugly — or beautiful — depending on your zip code.



State
Approx. State Tax
Annual Take-Home




Texas, Florida, Nevada (0%)
$0
$78,736


Colorado (4.4%)
~$3,740
$74,996


Illinois (4.95%)
~$4,208
$74,528


New York (6.0-6.85%)
~$5,100
$73,636


California (9.3%)
~$5,600
$73,136



In a zero-tax state, you're looking at $78,736 after federal taxes and FICA. That's $6,561 per month.
In California or New York, you're closer to $73,000–$74,000 annually. That's $6,083–$6,167

zh.title: 预算细分: 税后10万美元薪资 — 你并不富有
zh.summary: 10万美元总薪资在零税州只能获得78,736美元年度税后收入（或在高税州为73,000-74,000美元），需扣除联邦所得税（13,614美元）、FICA税（7,650美元）和州所得税。这相当于在有利州每月约6,561美元税前，或在高税地区约6,083-6,167美元，还未计入房租和生活费用。该分析表明六位数薪资提供舒适的中产阶级生活而非财富。

fr.title: Détail du budget: Salaire de 100 000 $ après impôts - Vous n'êtes pas riche
fr.summary: Un salaire brut de 100 000 $ ne donne que 78 736 $ de revenus annuels nets dans les États sans impôt sur le revenu (ou 73 000 à 74 000 $ dans les États à impôts élevés) après impôt fédéral sur le revenu (13 614 $), impôts FICA (7 650 $) et impôts sur le revenu des États. Cela se décompose à environ 6 561 $ / mois avant impôts dans les États favorables ou environ 6 083 à 6 167 $ / mois dans les zones à fiscalité élevée, avant loyer et frais de subsistance. L'analyse montre que les salaires à six chiffres offrent un mode de vie confortable de la classe moyenne plutôt que la richesse.

de.title: Budgetaufschlüsselung: $100.000 Gehalt nach Steuern - Du bist nicht reich
de.summary: Ein Bruttogehalt von 100.000 $ ergibt nur 78.736 $ jährliches Nettoeinkommen in steuerfreien Bundesstaaten (oder 73.000-74.000 $ in Hochsteuerstaaten) nach Bundeseinkommensteuer (13.614 $), FICA-Steuern (7.650 $) und Landeseinkommensteuer. Dies entspricht etwa 6.561 $ / Monat vor Steuern in günstigen Staaten oder etwa 6.083-6.167 $ / Monat in Hochsteuergebieten, vor Miete und Lebenshaltungskosten. Die Analyse zeigt, dass sechsstellige Gehälter einen komfortablen Mittelschicht-Lebensstil bieten, keine Reichtum.

es.title: Desglose presupuestario: Salario de $100K después de impuestos — No eres rico
es.summary: Un salario bruto de $100K produce solo $78,736 de ingresos anuales netos en estados sin impuestos (o $73,000-$74,000 en estados con impuestos altos) después del impuesto federal sobre la renta ($13,614), impuestos FICA ($7,650) e impuestos estatales sobre la renta. Esto se divide en aproximadamente $6,561/mes antes de impuestos en estados favorables o aproximadamente $6,083-$6,167/mes en áreas de impuestos altos, antes de alquiler y gastos de vida. El análisis demuestra que los salarios de seis dígitos proporcionan una vida cómoda de la clase media en lugar de riqueza.

title: Building a Terminal Text Editor: The View (Part 3)
summary: This third part of a terminal text editor series covers the View component, which handles user input collection and rendering of text, warnings, word counts, and file status without making logic decisions. The author chose FTXUI, a modern C++20 library, over traditional ncurses for building interactive terminal interfaces with component-based design. The View implements an abstract interface and receives all rendering instructions from the Presenter, maintaining separation of concerns in the Model-View-Presenter architecture.
content:
Recap


In Part 1, I talked about the origin of this project, the early architectural decisions, and why I picked the GapBuffer with IBuffer as the contract for all future buffer implementations. In Part 2, we got into the details of the Presenter, how it wires the components together, handles user input, manages state, and keeps the Model and View in sync. Now we can move on to the View.
The View is the component that the user interacts with directly. All user inputs are collected and passed to the Presenter, the View doesn't decide what to do with them. It is also responsible for rendering the text the user types, along with any warnings, statistics like word count, and whether the file is unsaved. The View doesn't calculate any of this, it just displays whatever the Presenter sends it. Another example of how all three parts of the Model-View-Presenter separate concerns.
In the proof of concept testing, I used ncurses, the de facto standard library for building terminal interfaces. It worked well for the initial testing, and I see a lot of projects still using it; it's tried and true. While ncurses would have been a great choice, I did a bit more research and found FTXUI, a modern C++ library with components to build interactive terminal user interfaces. I wanted to leverage that it uses modern C++, matching the project's early defined specification of targeting C++20.
While implementing the other components (Model and Presenter), I mocked up a thin interface for the View, IView. At first, it was only taking character input and not accounting for special characters, and it didn't handle rendering any data. The proof of concept View just took the input, sent it to the Presenter, which would send it to the buffer and log it so I could see the data was actually flowing through the system.
I was thinking of removing it, but after doing more research, I decided to expand the interface instead. Keeping IView as an abstract class means that if someone wants to write thei

zh.title: 构建终端文本编辑器：视图（第3部分）
zh.summary: 这是终端文本编辑器系列的第三部分，涵盖处理用户输入收集和文本渲染、警告、字数和文件状态的View组件，而不做出逻辑决策。作者选择了现代C++20库FTXUI，而不是传统ncurses，用于构建具有基于组件设计的交互式终端界面。View实现了一个抽象接口，从Presenter接收所有渲染指令，在Model-View-Presenter架构中保持关注点分离。

fr.title: Construire un éditeur de texte terminal : la vue (partie 3)
fr.summary: Cette troisième partie d'une série d'éditeurs de texte en terminal couvre le composant View qui gère la collecte des entrées utilisateur et le rendu du texte, des avertissements, des comptages de mots et du statut du fichier sans prendre de décisions logiques. L'auteur a choisi FTXUI, une bibliothèque C++20 moderne, plutôt que ncurses traditionnel pour construire des interfaces terminales interactives avec une conception basée sur les composants. La View implémente une interface abstraite et reçoit toutes les instructions de rendu du Presenter, en maintenant la séparation des préoccupations dans l'architecture Model-View-Presenter.

de.title: Einen Terminal-Text-Editor erstellen: Die Ansicht (Teil 3)
de.summary: Dieser dritte Teil einer Terminal-Text-Editor-Serie behandelt die View-Komponente, die Benutzereingabesammlung und das Rendern von Text, Warnungen, Wortanzahl und Dateistatus verwaltet, ohne logische Entscheidungen zu treffen. Der Autor wählte FTXUI, eine moderne C++20-Bibliothek, gegenüber traditionellem ncurses zum Erstellen von interaktiven Terminal-Schnittstellen mit komponentenbasiertem Design. Die View implementiert eine abstrakte Schnittstelle und erhält alle Rendering-Anweisungen vom Presenter und wahrt die Trennung der Anliegen in der Model-View-Presenter-Architektur.

es.title: Construyendo un editor de texto de terminal: la vista (parte 3)
es.summary: Esta tercera parte de una serie de editor de texto de terminal cubre el componente Vista, que maneja la recopilación de entrada del usuario y la representación de texto, advertencias, conteos de palabras y estado del archivo sin tomar decisiones lógicas. El autor eligió FTXUI, una biblioteca C++20 moderna, sobre ncurses tradicional para construir interfaces de terminal interactivas con diseño basado en componentes. La Vista implementa una interfaz abstracta y recibe todas las instrucciones de representación del Presentador, manteniendo la separación de responsabilidades en la arquitectura Modelo-Vista-Presentador.
